albums.title: Homework
albums.reason: With nearly 3,000 all-time plays, Daft Punk are your most-loved artist ever — and Homework is where it all began, the raw house-inflected record before the polish of Discovery.

albums.title: Kid A
albums.reason: Reckoner and True Love Waits both sit in your all-time top tracks, and Kid A is the album where Radiohead pushed deepest into the electronic wilderness you clearly loved.

albums.title: Premiers Symptômes
albums.reason: Sexy Boy and La femme d'argent are both in your all-time top 50 tracks, and this early Air EP compilation captures exactly the sleepy, cinematic mood that made them a staple for you.

albums.title: Hot Fuss
albums.reason: Mr. Brightside, A Dustland Fairytale, and All These Things That I've Done all appear in your all-time top tracks — Hot Fuss is where that Killers obsession was born.

albums.title: Orbital (Brown Album)
albums.reason: Orbital clock 777 all-time plays for you and Don't Stop Me is a favourite track — this second album is their most hypnotic and transportive, and it's been a long time.

albums.title: Protection
albums.reason: Massive Attack are a long-standing pillar of your listening history, and Protection is their most tender and underplayed record — the one that deserves a deep revisit.

albums.title: Melody A.M.
albums.reason: Röyksopp sit at over 1,000 all-time plays and The Girl and the Robot is a favourite track — Melody A.M. is their warm, pillowy debut and likely a record you haven't properly revisited in years.

albums.title: The North Borders
albums.reason: Bonobo have been active across both your all-time and recent 12-month listening, and The North Borders sits just outside your recent 3-month window — rich, layered, and overdue a relisten.

albums.title: Assume Form
albums.reason: James Blake tops your all-time track chart with Postpone on 156 plays, and Assume Form is his most emotionally open record — released recently enough to feel current, but not something you've been spinning lately.

albums.title: II
albums.reason: Moderat's Rusty Nails appears in your all-time top tracks and you've loved their full catalogue — II is their most cohesive and affecting album, sitting perfectly in the mid-era sweet spot this tier is built for.

albums.title: Fabric Presents Maribou State
albums.reason: Maribou State have 434 all-time plays and Beginner's Luck is a favourite — this DJ mix showcases exactly the warm, deep house side of them that fits your medium-term listening arc.

albums.title: Rounds
albums.reason: Four Tet have been consistent across your recent 12-month listening and your all-time history — Rounds is their defining record and likely hasn't been on your rotation for a while.

albums.title: Idealism
albums.reason: You have Justice and Daft Punk deep in your all-time DNA — Digitalism occupy the same French-influenced electro space and Idealism is a cult classic that slots perfectly between those two worlds.

albums.title: Psyence Fiction
albums.reason: Your love of Massive Attack, Moby, and Leftfield points squarely at late-90s big-beat and trip-hop — Unkle's debut is a wild, star-studded record from that exact scene that you've probably overlooked.

albums.title: Fixion
albums.reason: Wait — Trentemøller is already in your data. Let me correct: Röyksopp's melodic electronics and your love of Plaid point toward Jóhann Jóhannsson. His album Orphée is a quietly devastating ambient-classical record.

albums.title: Orphée
albums.reason: Your deep love of Plaid, Aphex Twin, and Air's cinematic side points here — Orphée is a hushed, transcendent record that lives in the same emotional space as your most introspective listening.
With Radiohead, Pink Floyd, and Leftfield all deep in your all-time list, you clearly love music with propulsive, hypnotic structure — Beak>'s second album is exactly that, a krautrock cult classic.

albums.title: R.I.P.
albums.reason: Your love of Aphex Twin, Plaid, and Modeselektor signals a real appetite for challenging electronic music — Actress's R.I.P. is one of the most acclaimed records in that space and likely a blind spot for you.

albums.title: Swim
albums.reason: MGMT, The Avalanches, and your love of lysergic electronic textures all point toward Caribou — Swim is a modern classic of shimmering, beat-driven psychedelia that sits beautifully in your all-time lane.

albums.title: No Home of the Mind
albums.reason: Your heavy recent listening to Nils Frahm, Hania Rani, and Joep Beving shows a real appetite for contemplative piano music — Bing & Ruth's minimalist, slowly evolving pieces are a perfect next step.

albums.title: Man Made Object
albums.reason: Portico Quartet and the Oscar Peterson Trio both sit high in your recent 12-month listening — GoGo Penguin bridge jazz structure with electronic sensibility in a way that feels tailor-made for where you are now.

albums.title: Elaenia
albums.reason: Your recent arc — Portico Quartet, Four Tet, Bonobo, DJ Koze — lands squarely in the jazz-meets-electronic space, and Floating Points' Elaenia is one of the defining albums of exactly that intersection.

albums.title: Articulation
albums.reason: Your recent listening to Ben Lukas Boysen, Apparat, and Max Cooper points toward emotionally intelligent electronic music with real texture — Articulation is a stunning record in that same vein.

albums.title: Your Queen Is a Reptile
albums.reason: Sault, Charles Bradley, and your broader recent soul and jazz explorations suggest you'd connect with Sons of Kemet's raw, politically charged jazz — this Mercury Prize-nominated album is a landmark.

albums.title: The Return
albums.reason: Your recent listening to Portico Quartet, St Germain, and the Oscar Peterson Trio draws a clear line to jazz with soul and groove — Kamaal Williams's debut is warm, hypnotic, and deeply in that pocket.

albums.title: Texas Sun
albums.reason: Khruangbin are dominating your listening right now with 47 plays this month and May Ninth and Mariella on heavy repeat — Texas Sun is the perfect companion EP to A La Sala that you haven't been spinning lately.

albums.title: Steelpan Dreams
albums.reason: Portico Quartet are your second most-played artist this month and Ruins is on constant repeat — this early record captures the same open, resonant quality you're clearly craving right now.

albums.title: Black Classical Music
albums.reason: Birds of Paradise just appeared in your most recent tracks this week — diving into the full album feels like the natural move right now.

albums.title: Esto Es Hermanos Gutiérrez
albums.reason: Hermanos Gutiérrez are your third most-played artist this month and you've been deep in their catalogue — this recent compilation covers their essential work and fits the warm, drifting mood you're in.

albums.title: Dial M for Monkey
albums.reason: Bonobo have 14 plays this month and you've been in a warm, organic headspace — Dial M for Monkey is their most lo-fi and soulful record, and it hasn't appeared in your recent rotation.

albums.title: Gold
albums.reason: Cleo Sol appeared in your listening this month and the warm, intimate soul direction you're in right now — Sault, Bill Withers, Charles Bradley — makes Gold a perfect fit for this exact moment.

songs.title: Da Funk
songs.reason: With Daft Punk sitting at the very top of your all-time plays, Da Funk is one of their most iconic early cuts — raw, distorted, and a world away from the polished tracks that dominate your top track list.

songs.title: How to Disappear Completely
songs.reason: Reckoner and True Love Waits are both deep in your all-time top tracks — How to Disappear Completely from Kid A is Radiohead at their most hauntingly beautiful and deserves a revisit.

songs.title: Comfortably Numb
songs.reason: Pink Floyd sit at nearly 1,200 all-time plays for you — Comfortably Numb is the guitar monument at the heart of The Wall, and it's the kind of track that earns its place in any deep nostalgia pool.

songs.title: D.A.N.C.E.
songs.reason: Justice have 771 all-time plays and both Phantom Pt. II and Genesis sit in your all-time top tracks — D.A.N.C.E. is the joyful counterpoint to their darker cuts and a genuine piece of nostalgia.

songs.title: Block Rockin' Beats
songs.reason: The Chemical Brothers have 556 all-time plays and sit firmly in your deep electronic roots — Block Rockin' Beats is one of the definitive tracks of that whole era and a pure nostalgia hit.

songs.title: Rez
songs.reason: Underworld have 633 all-time plays and Born Slippy is a favourite — Rez is their other defining epic, a slow-building techno anthem that's the perfect deep vault counterpart.

songs.title: Near Light
songs.reason: Ólafur Arnalds had 28 plays in your last 12 months and Near Light was already recommended as a song you liked — but the studio version from ...And They Have Escaped the Weight of Darkness is an all-timer worth returning to.

songs.title: Moan
songs.reason: Trentemøller sit at 560 all-time plays for you — Moan from The Last Resort is the brooding centrepiece of that debut and represents a recent-vault era that your data confirms you loved heavily.

songs.title: Kiara
songs.reason: Bonobo are active across your all-time and recent listening, and Kiara from Black Sands is one of their most emotionally resonant moments — a track that sits perfectly in the medium-term vault.

songs.title: Porcelain
songs.reason: Moby sits at 551 all-time plays — Porcelain from Play is his most delicate and enduring track, and it occupies that recent-vault sweet spot of music you loved deeply but haven't revisited in a while.

songs.title: Frontier Psychiatrist
songs.reason: The Avalanches sit at 505 all-time plays — Frontier Psychiatrist is their most audacious and joyful cut, the kind of track that defined a whole era of your listening.

songs.title: Windowlicker
songs.reason: Aphex Twin have 522 all-time plays and Xtal is a favourite track — Windowlicker is the wild counterpoint: unsettling, brilliant, and a track that belongs in a vault revisit.

songs.title: Zdarlight
songs.reason: Your all-time love of Justice and Daft Punk's harder electro side maps perfectly to Digitalism — Zdarlight is one of the great overlooked anthems of that French-influenced electro-house era.

songs.title: The Sun's Gone Dim
songs.reason: Your deep love of Air's atmospheric side and Plaid's emotional textures points directly at Jóhannsson — this track from IBM 1401 is devastating and beautiful in equal measure.

songs.title: Wulfstan II
songs.reason: Your Radiohead and Leftfield listening history suggests you love music that locks into a hypnotic groove — Wulfstan II is Beak>'s most compelling track and one of the best kept secrets in post-rock.

songs.title: Ascending
songs.reason: Your Aphex Twin and Modeselektor listening history shows a clear appetite for challenging, abstract electronics — Ascending from R.I.P. is Actress at his most arresting and oddly beautiful.

songs.title: Odessa
songs.reason: MGMT's Time to Pretend is in your all-time top tracks and you clearly love lysergic, layered pop — Odessa from Swim is Caribou's most indelible moment, a genuine lost classic for your taste.

songs.title: Rabbit in Your Headlights
songs.reason: Your deep connection to Massive Attack and Moby's more cinematic moments makes this UNKLE classic essential — Rabbit in Your Headlights is one of the most powerful trip-hop tracks ever made.

songs.title: LesAlpx
songs.reason: Your recent deep listening to Portico Quartet, Four Tet, and Bonobo all converge on the jazz-meets-electronic space — LesAlpx from Elaenia is one of the most stunning tracks at that intersection.

songs.title: Starwood Choker
songs.reason: Your recent heavy play of Nils Frahm, Hania Rani, and Joep Beving maps directly to Bing & Ruth's world — Starwood Choker is hypnotic, slowly shifting, and exactly what you've been seeking.

songs.title: Stones Throw
songs.reason: Your recent listening to the Oscar Peterson Trio and Portico Quartet shows a growing appetite for jazz with real warmth and swing — this track from The Return hits that feeling perfectly.

songs.title: My Queen Is Ada Eastman
songs.reason: Sault, Charles Bradley, and Syl Johnson have all been in heavy rotation for you recently — Sons of Kemet channel that same soulful, politically charged energy into raw, exhilarating jazz.

songs.title: Hopopono
songs.reason: Your recent Oscar Peterson Trio and Portico Quartet listening makes it clear you're drawn to jazz with structure and momentum — Hopopono is GoGo Penguin's most thrilling single track.

songs.title: Unfolding
songs.reason: Your recent Ben Lukas Boysen, Apparat, and Brian Eno listening points to music that's emotionally intelligent and texturally rich — Unfolding from Articulation is a masterclass in exactly that.

songs.title: Connaissais de Face
songs.reason: You've been looping Mariella and May Ninth all week — Connaissais de Face from Con Todo El Mundo is in the same slow, floating headspace and deserves to be next in the queue.

songs.title: Steelpan Dreams
songs.reason: Ruins has been on constant repeat this week and you're clearly deep in the Portico Quartet world — Steelpan Dreams is their earliest recorded magic, a hushed and beautiful place to go deeper.

songs.title: Sun Kissed Child
songs.reason: Birds of Paradise just appeared in your most recent plays this week — Sun Kissed Child from the same album has the same luminous, searching quality and feels like the ideal follow-on.

songs.title: Caminando
songs.reason: Hermanos Gutiérrez are your third most-played artist this month and you're clearly in their world — Caminando captures the same sun-bleached, unhurried mood you've been living in.

songs.title: Nightlite
songs.reason: Bonobo have 14 plays this month and you're in a warm, organic groove — Nightlite from Dial M for Monkey is one of their most soulful tracks and fits the late-night haze you're sitting in.

songs.title: Shrewsbury Fair
songs.reason: Jake Xerxes Fussell appeared in your listening just this week alongside Frolic — Shrewsbury Fair from the same album carries that same dusty, unhurried, ancient-feeling warmth.

djSets.setName: HMWL Podcast 430: Niconé & David Hasert (Kompakt / Get Physical / HMWL)
Daphni - FOLD, London. Nov 22, 2025. 7hr DJ set
Klausmarie b2b Alec Troniq - RAVIN' RAVIN' 2026 @ Sektor Evolution
